INST1A:
Math Processing Task

In this task, you must solve simple addition and subtraction problems to determine whether the correct answer is greater than or less than 5.

The two possible responses are:
- Less than 5 (left response)
- Greater than 5 (right response)

No problem will ever have the value 5 as the correct answer.

Press any key to continue.
INST1B:
You start each block by pressing any key. Each block lasts for 3 minutes. Math problems appear one at a time, and should be solved from left to right. Always perform the additions and subtractions in the order they appear.

As soon as you respond, a new problem will appear. Try to be as fast and accurate as possible. If you start making errors because you are going too fast, slow down.

At the end of each 3-minute block, the task will automatically stop.

Press any key to continue.
INST1C:
The number of operations in each problem will vary between blocks. Some blocks will have problems with 2 numbers, others with 3 or 4 numbers. Within a block, all problems have the same number of operations.

Press any key to begin.
INTERBLOCK:
The problems in this block will all have numbers. You have a deadline of seconds to solve each one.

Press any key to begin.
DEBRIEF: This test is complete. Press any key to continue.
